mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month without requiring specific macroeconomic thresholds.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment levels signals weakening aggregate demand, prompting equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 forward returns once the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the PAYEMS decline signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 3% within 5 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns when both series move inversely.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus HOUST growth) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or positive SPX returns dominate during confirmed bottleneck periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising short-term policy rates combined with accelerating consumer price inflation compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns specifically during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in months with positive payroll growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in real consumer credit outstanding signals tightening financial conditions and reduced household purchasing power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns once the decline threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the RSAFS contraction signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5% within 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that lifts cyclical equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns when both series confirm growth.
failure_condition: The interaction term (GDPC1 growth plus HOUST growth) shows no predictive power for 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or negative SPX returns dominate during confirmed expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated short-term interest rates during post-shock recovery phases anchor long-term yield expectations higher, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns until policy normalization completes.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS stays elevated during post-shock recovery phases, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 3% within 5 trading days.
